asset_type: TaraHome technical blog cover, landscape hero and 16:9 article-card crop
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome technical blog cover, landscape hero and 16:9 article-card crop
Primary request: a clean editorial photograph that conveys one connected smart home spanning several rooms, for an article about Thread 1.4 and whether different smart-home hubs can share one mesh
Scene/backdrop: a real warm residential interior with a living room opening naturally into a hallway and kitchen, continuous sightline through the home, natural oak, limewashed walls, wool and ceramic textures
Subject: a homeowner moving through the connected rooms while casually holding a phone with the screen turned away; ordinary built-in lights are on in each room, creating a subtle sense that the whole house works together
Style/medium: high-end but believable architectural lifestyle photography, natural texture, restrained editorial polish
Composition/framing: wide horizontal composition, strong room-to-room depth, main human figure near the center third, important content safe inside a 16:9 center crop and still readable as a small card
Lighting/mood: warm late-afternoon window light, calm and practical rather than futuristic
Color palette: warm cream, oak, muted clay, restrained dark-green accents from upholstery and real houseplants
Constraints: no visible branded or invented smart-home product hardware; no readable phone screen; no text; no logos; no watermark; no embedded headline or poster; no fake chart or graph; no decorative routing, network, signal, path, or connection-line overlays; no translucent panels; no inset frames; no floating UI cards; no progress bars; no split-screen; no diagram; no sci-fi glow
Avoid: generic AI-looking smart-home diagrams, staged catalog perfection, impossible architecture, duplicated objects, distorted hands, excessive plants, cool blue lighting
